articles.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns of 'reverse information paradox' facing businesses in AI Age

articles.description: As the risk of an El Niño looms over the kharif season, India's largest foodgrain stockpile in five years gives the government a cushion against weather-led supply disruptions. The higher reserves also provide greater flexibility to curb food inflation and meet welfare needs.

articles.description: Strong winds and heavy rain are expected to impact many eastern and northeastern Chinese cities on Sunday (July 12) and Monday (July 13), China's National Meteorological Centre said

articles.description: The INDIA bloc plans to confront the government on key issues during the upcoming Parliament session. They will raise concerns about the Ram Mandir donation case and the NEET examination paper leak. Opposition parties also intend to resist alleged BJP efforts to engineer defections and weaken rivals. This strategy aims to secure a two-thirds majority for the ruling party's legislative agenda.

articles.title: 'If not in Delhi, then where?' : Omar Abdullah announces statehood protest on July 20
articles.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah announced fresh protests in Delhi starting July 20. These demonstrations aim to address the unexplained delay in restoring statehood to the Union Territory. Abdullah stated that his party has waited two years after the Assembly elections for this restoration. He questioned the Centre's repeated deferral of statehood to an appropriate time. The National Conference will continue its campaign through peaceful and democratic means.

articles.title: Omar defends invite to Mirwaiz for Delhi protest, says BJP creating 'manufactured controversy'
articles.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah defended inviting Mirwaiz Umar Farooq to a Delhi protest. He accused the BJP and media of creating controversy to divert attention from statehood restoration. Abdullah questioned the BJP's claims about separatism's elimination in Kashmir. He stated the invitation controversy was manufactured to shift public focus. The large rally demonstrated strong public commitment to restoring Jammu and Kashmir's statehood.

articles.title: Why it’s absurd and unfair to drag Indian business-family culture into a case of murder
articles.description: Business family stereotypes are particularly unjust when invoked in the context of a grave crime. Such generalized notions don’t withstand scrutiny. Yet, commentators have sought to offer a cultural explanation for the death of a young man.

articles.title: TMC's Abhishek Banerjee booked after woman loses leg, blames treatment at Sebaashray health camp
articles.description: TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee faces a new FIR after a woman lost her leg. Maloti Biswas complained of medical negligence at a Sebaashray health camp. She attended the camp for knee pain and was prescribed medicines. Her condition worsened, and doctors demanded money for further treatment. The woman's leg was amputated after being referred to a government hospital.

articles.title: What a dolphin’s death in Kerala reveals about India’s marine plastic pollution
articles.description: Necropsy of an endangered Indian Ocean humpback dolphin that washed ashore on Kozhikode beach recently reveals fatal plastic fishing net ingestion, highlighting growing threats from marine pollution and ‘ghost nets’. CMFRI data show marine mammal strandings remain high, with coastal dolphins among the species most vulnerable

articles.description: The Chinese regime is unhappy about women adopting AI companions in a country hoping to raise its birth rate, but state intervention can’t achieve much without addressing the concrete social conditions driving demand for such bots.

Singh expressed deep anguish over ministerial loose talk concerning the poll watchdog's functioning. He emphasized the Election Commission's vital role as the soul of democracy. The former Prime Minister offered to commit suicide if the institution was undermined.

articles.description: Kerala’s approach to science has changed, with basic research losing its place among the public policy priorities. The State is understandably attracted to projects that deliver visible and immediate outcomes, but that does not mean abdicating fundamental scientific research is okay. Only such research can lead to long-term value creation

articles.description: Just over a week after Fable 5 came back online for all users, OpenAI unveiled its own next-generation family: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna. Rather than a single flagship, OpenAI split the release into three capability tiers priced separately with Sol at the top and Luna at the bottom, arguing this lets each tier advance on its own tempo.

articles.title: Omar claims BJP luring NC MLAs with cash and ministerial berths in Jammu and Kashmir
articles.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah accused the BJP of trying to lure his MLAs. He claimed the party offered money and ministerial posts to engineer a split. Abdullah stated that the BJP is now promising statehood to National Conference legislators. He asserted that his party's MLAs will not compromise their integrity for financial gain.

articles.title: Cracking joints, back pain, weak bones? Orthopaedic experts reveal warning signs
articles.description: Orthopaedic experts report rising musculoskeletal disorders in those under 30, attributing them to modern lifestyles. They emphasize the importance of early intervention and healthy habits, like regular exercise and balanced diets, to support long-term bone and joint health.

articles.title: Nitin Gadkari calls for stronger BRICS ties for sustainable and future-ready transport systems
articles.description: Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways, Nitin Gadkari, on Sunday called for deeper cooperation among BRICS nations to build transport systems that are sustainable, resilient, inclusive and future-ready, stating that the collective strength of the grouping presents a unique opportunity to shape the future of global mobility through innovation, partnership and shared responsibility.

articles.title: MP Congress chief Jitu Patwari's brother questioned for second time in drug trafficking probe
articles.description: Indore police questioned Kulbhushan alias Nana Patwari for a second time this week. He is being investigated in connection with a drug trafficking case. Two arrested individuals allegedly named Patwari as a recipient of narcotics. Patwari claims he was targeted due to his brother's political position. Police continue their investigation into the alleged drug network.

articles.title: 'Scam Altman' vs 'homeboy': Elon Musk and Sam Altman lock horns after Apple lawsuit
articles.description: Elon Musk and Sam Altman reignited their public feud after Apple sued OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft. Musk accused Altman of "scamming" in a series of posts on X. Altman, in turn, responded by mocking Musk's plans for space-based data centres to power AI.

articles.title: Bluesky appoints Toni Schneider as CEO after four-month interim stint
articles.description: Toni Schneider has been named Bluesky's permanent chief executive officer. He previously served in an interim capacity for four months. Schneider replaced longtime CEO Jay Graber in March, when Graber moved into the role of chief innovation officer while continuing as chair of Bluesky's board.

articles.title: UBT Sena MP Sanjay Raut fuels Devendra Fadnavis Delhi shift buzz, triggers NDA tussle
articles.description: Sanjay Raut fueled speculation about Devendra Fadnavis moving to Delhi. Bachchu Kadu suggested Eknath Shinde should become chief minister then. Kadu stated Maharashtra would progress faster with Fadnavis in Delhi. BJP MLC Pravin Darekar dismissed Kadu's comments as childish remarks. Darekar emphasised the alliance is working happily without controversy.

articles.title: Parliament panel proposes suspension, not removal, of jailed ministers
articles.description: A parliamentary panel suggests suspending ministers instead of removing them from office. This change ensures innocent individuals are not permanently deprived of their positions. The committee also proposed defining serious criminal offenses to narrow the bill's scope. Automatic reversal of suspension is recommended if prosecution fails within a set period. Fast-track courts are favored for trying cases involving constitutional functionaries.

articles.title: BJP looks to steady Bankipur campaign after candidate change
articles.description: The BJP faces a challenge in Bankipur after its initial candidate withdrew his nomination. Neeraj Kumar Sinha is now the party's announced candidate for the prestigious seat. This quick change has provided ammunition for the opposition parties. The BJP plans its campaign carefully to manage fallout and focus on voters. Neeraj Sinha will file his nomination on Monday with NDA leaders present.

articles.title: Owaisi urges Telangana to issue residence certificates ahead of voter roll revision
articles.description: Asaduddin Owaisi requested Telangana's Congress government to issue Permanent Residence Certificates. This move aims to prevent residents from electoral roll deletions during the revision exercise. Karnataka's Congress government recently issued similar certificates to its residents. The BJP has raised constitutional and national security concerns regarding this initiative. Union minister Shobha Karandlaje has sought intervention from the Union Home Minister.

articles.title: Narottam Mishra urges calm after supporters protest BJP ticket denial
articles.description: Supporters of former MLA Narottam Mishra protested violently after he was denied a party ticket. Police dispersed the demonstrators, and several district office-bearers resigned from their positions. Mishra urged his workers to accept the BJP's decision and abide by party directives. He appealed to his supporters to avoid violence and use internal party channels. The party leadership confirmed the new candidate would not be replaced, which Mishra accepted.

articles.title: J&K CM Omar Abdullah alleges BJP bid to split NC, claims party MLA was offered Rs 20–30 cr to switch sides
Punjab Congress rift deepens: Baghel peace meet fails to signal thaw as Channi, Randhawa say exit of Warring must for party unity
articles.description: Punjab Congress change seekers met with AICC in-charge Bhupesh Baghel on Saturday. They reiterated their demand for the removal of PCC president Amarinder Singh Raja Warring. Baghel assured them he would convey their issues to the party high command. Warring responded by stating compromised leaders cannot remain in the party. The meeting failed to resolve the ongoing internal party tensions.

articles.title: Omar Abdullah alleges BJP offered NC MLAs Rs 20-30 crore to defect
articles.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah accused the BJP of attempting to destabilize his government. He claimed that BJP leaders offered money and ministerial positions to National Conference MLAs. One MLA reported being offered twenty to thirty crore rupees and statehood. Abdullah expressed confidence that his party legislators would not be swayed by such offers. NC President Farooq Abdullah also acknowledged attempts to break the party.

articles.description: The Ritabrata Banerjee-led rebel TMC faction has unveiled parallel state and district committees. Longtime loyalists, including Anubrata Mondal, have joined the rebel group, signaling institutional building. This move signifies a shift beyond challenging Mamata Banerjee's leadership and towards a parallel party apparatus. The faction is also seeking recognition as the "real" Trinamool Congress from the Election Commission.

articles.title: Just Dial CEO VSS Mani to step down; former Flipkart executive Dinkar Ayilavarapu named successor
articles.description: Just Dial founder VSS Mani will step down as CEO on July 31, 2026. Dinkar Ayilavarapu, formerly of Flipkart, will assume the CEO role on August 1. Mani founded Just Dial in 1996 and has led the company for nearly three decades, overseeing its evolution into one of India's largest local search and discovery platforms.

iOS 27: Apple Mail gets AI Search, Smart Replies and Siri features
SoftBank names ex-Goldman Sachs trader Mark Agne to lead Vision Fund finances
articles.description: SoftBank Vision Fund has appointed Mark Agne to lead finance and technology roles. The move follows a series of staffing changes at SoftBank as its focus shifts alongside founder Masayoshi Son’s quest for technologies underpinning AI services. Agne joined SoftBank in 2019 after 17 years at Goldman Sachs, where he led the Japan securities division and was co-head of Asia-Pacific equity trading.

articles.title: Mighty Mac: the longest suspension bridge in the western hemisphere
articles.description: June 28, 1958 marks the end of a four-day party for the dedication festival of the Mackinac Bridge. Even though the bridge had been opened to traffic from November the previous year, it was only from June 25-28 that the official festivities and ribbon-cutting had taken place. Join A.S.Ganesh as he takes you on a trip to tell you about this suspension bridge…

Global financial technology companies are increasingly turning to the country for product engineering, artificial intelligence and cross-border infrastructure. The country's fintech sector is estimated at USD 111 billion and is projected to reach USD 421 billion by 2029.

Since then, she has travelled to Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Uttarakhand, Rajasthan and Tamil Nadu in search of birds and has captured images of 300 to 400 species of birds so far.

articles.title: Swiggy Instamart gets 9 FSSAI notices following customer complaints on contaminated food, milk
articles.description: FSSAI has issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart due to multiple consumer complaints. Allegations include supplying expired, spoiled, and contaminated food products to customers. Specific products like eggs and protein powder were found unfit for consumption. The regulator also noted issues with FSSAI license numbers and grievance redressal. Swiggy Instamart must submit a detailed report or face legal action.

articles.description: The nearly 12-hour blockade on NH-44 by over 3,000 protesters left the Datia superintendent of police and several cops injured before security forces cleared the highway and arrested some agitators, officials said.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI for stealing trade secrets
articles.description: The suit will significantly complicate OpenAI's plans for a hotly anticipated initial public offering.

articles.title: Urban Company CHRO Neha Mathur resigns
articles.description: Urban Company's chief human resources officer Neha Mathur has resigned from her position. Mathur tendered her resignation on July tenth for personal reasons and family matters. She spent approximately five years leading the people success function at the company. Mathur expressed her belief that Urban Company is destined for enduring institutional success.

Bill Gates heir’s shopping app claimed sales it didn’t drive
articles.description: Phia, co-founded by Phoebe Gates, faces accusations of violating affiliate marketing policies. The browser extension allegedly claimed sales it did not drive by injecting its own referral codes. Independent researchers and competitors like Capital One Shopping highlighted these concerning practices. Phia acknowledged the issue, stating it has been fixed and resolved. The company has previously faced allegations regarding user data logging practices.

articles.title: Meta AI image detector fails to identify some of its own cropped AI images: Report
Meta scraps AI image feature days after launch following privacy backlash
articles.description: Meta withdrew its new AI image generation feature after significant criticism. Meta's Muse image model provides a feature that lets users generate and edit images using natural language prompts like most AI image generators. However, it drew major criticism from privacy advocates, creators and entertainment industry groups given that it also allowed users to create AI-generated images using public Instagram photos without their explicit consent.

articles.title: Meta appeals verdict in social media addiction lawsuit
articles.description: Meta has appealed a landmark social media addiction lawsuit verdict in Los Angeles. The company challenges the jury's determination that its platforms were designed to hook young users. This appeal follows a jury finding negligence by Meta and Google's YouTube. The jury awarded a young woman three million dollars in damages. Both companies plan to appeal, citing the complexity of teen mental health issues.

articles.title: Weight‑loss drugs vs bariatric surgery: Who should choose what, experts explain
articles.description: Obesity care is shifting from one‑size‑fits‑all to tailored regimens. Experts explain how weight‑loss medicines like GLP‑1s and bariatric surgery complement each other, with treatment decisions guided by BMI, comorbidities, patient history and long‑term health goals.

articles.title: UK to regulate cloud service providers Microsoft, Google and others to protect financial stability
articles.description: The UK government has identified Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Oracle as essential cloud providers, implying new regulatory measures for these tech giants. This initiative is designed to bolster the resilience of the financial sector against potential cyber threats and service interruptions, introducing stringent testing and reporting protocols. Through this framework, the UK aims to secure and stabilize its financial infrastructure for the future.

articles.title: OpenAI shutters ChatGPT Atlas, moves browser features to the new ChatGPT Work
articles.description: OpenAI is discontinuing its ChatGPT Atlas browser less than a year after its launch. The company is integrating Atlas's features into its new ChatGPT Work desktop application. This move consolidates browser and AI capabilities into a single platform for users. Atlas helped shape the agent and browsing functionalities now present in ChatGPT Work. OpenAI continues to streamline its product offerings by integrating successful features.

articles.title: China’s temporary export ban on helium| Explained

articles.title: OpenAI to shut down ChatGPT Atlas browser: Here’s why
EU tells Meta to change Facebook, Instagram's 'addictive design'
articles.description: The European Union has warned Meta to alter Facebook and Instagram's addictive design features. Brussels accuses the tech giant of failing to limit risks to users, especially children. Changes could include disabling features like infinite scroll and autoplay by default. Meta disagrees with the findings but will engage constructively with the EU. Failure to comply could result in fines up to six percent of Meta's global annual turnover.

articles.title: UK to regulate cloud service providers Microsoft, Google and others to protect financial stability
articles.description: Britain has designated Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Oracle as critical third parties. This move brings these major cloud providers under direct regulatory oversight. The government aims to protect the country's financial system from disruptions. Increased reliance on cloud services necessitates this regulatory action. These designations become effective from July thirteenth.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg’s Muse Spark post on X triggers debate, draws Elon Musk in
articles.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg returned to X after three years to announce Muse Spark 1.1. This AI model is designed for coding, reasoning, and agentic tasks. The announcement generated significant public discussion about the platform choice. Elon Musk commented that X is a great platform for direct CEO announcements. Users debated X's effectiveness for reaching the tech community compared to Threads.

articles.title: Finance ministry calls lenders for meeting on 13 July to review, step up foreign currency mobilization
articles.description: The government wants to boost the inflow of funds through foreign currency non-resident (bank) deposits, overseas foreign currency borrowings and external commercial borrowings.

Indian-origin NASA astronaut Anil Menon set for 8-month ISS mission: What to expect from his landmark journey
articles.description: Anil Menon, an astronaut of Indian origin, will embark on an eight-month ISS mission on July 14. He will also participate in testing new technologies that can generate intravenous (IV) fluids using the station's drinking water system. Menon will fly aboard Soyuz MS-29 with Russian cosmonauts.

articles.description: Meta launched Muse Image, an AI tool for image generation and editing. This feature allows users to create images using public Instagram photos. The feature has drawn privacy concerns because public posts and reels can be used by default to generate AI images, prompting questions around consent, misuse and user control over content

articles.title: India lays out tariffs and quotas for U.K. vehicles under trade deal
articles.description: As per the Directorate General of Foreign Trade’s notification, a total of 20,000 completely built units (CBUs) of petrol and diesel passenger vehicles will be allowed for import from the U.K. at concessional rates of 30-50%, depending on the size of the car, down from the normal import duty of between 66-110%

articles.title: One body, multiple pathways: where India is going wrong in regulating pesticide exposure
articles.description: When it comes to pesticide thresholds, India treats the pathways of food, water and air, as separate problems, each with its own laws and its own ceilings. No one asks what happens when all three pathways carry the same compounds into the same person on the same day
